title: Post-M2 roadmap - registered next steps for the single conductor
registered_by: user-approved proposal (scaffold M3/M5 after M2 seal, then real-test)
principle: Scaffold + test against REAL data (M2 sealed outputs). Never fabricate gate outcomes. Heavy tuning/statistical runs require an explicit go.

steps.action: Execute the disentangled .py consolidation per control/r0_migration/py_consolidation_plan.json (ONE driver -> ONE conductor -> phase runners -> ONE guard -> ONE executor + small core lib; retire rival drivers, merge duplicate friction).
steps.gated_by: M1_seal AND M2_seal AND explicit go (live-risky: import-entangled cluster).
steps.verify_gate: canonical chain import+compile clean AND verify-only PASS for M0/M1/M2 after every step (fail-closed).

steps.optimization_target: control/r0_migration/alpha_objective.json (daily-reinvested alpha beating daily momentum, net of cost)
steps.gated_by: M2_seal AND a real R0 tuning run (heavy compute, explicit go).

steps.action: Build tools/run_r0_migration_phase_m5.py: aggregate cost_stress/multiple_testing/robustness into evidence/r0_migration/gate_matrix.json (status PASS only if genuinely passing).
steps.gated_by: M3_seal AND real statistical analyses (heavy compute, explicit go).

notes: B-gates (integrity, M9 human approval, prerequisite seal chain) remain intact. This plan only removes friction by pre-registering automatable scaffolding work; it does not auto-advance past any safety gate.
